name: New England Lee
description_short: Federal credit union established in 1964 serving professional associations and trade organizations in Boston with savings accounts, auto loans, personal loans, and CDs.
description_long:
New England Lee Federal Credit Union was established in 1964 and is headquartered in Boston, Massachusetts. As a federally chartered credit union, it operates as a not-for-profit financial institution serving specialized professional and trade organization memberships rather than the general public.

The credit union offers a comprehensive range of retail banking products including regular savings accounts, share draft (checking) accounts, money market accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), IRA/KEOGH accounts, auto loans, personal loans, and mortgages. They provide financial education resources, online banking, mobile app access for both iOS and Android, online bill pay, and financial calculators to members. The institution maintains one physical branch location at 72 Kneeland Street in Boston and provides a routing number (211081083) for electronic transactions.

New England Lee distinguishes itself as a member-owned cooperative focused on professional associations and trade organizations, which defines their membership eligibility and lending practices. As a not-for-profit entity, they emphasize competitive interest rates and lower fees compared to commercial banks. They serve over 300 loyal members with approximately 2+ dedicated employees and steward $2.8 million in assets, indicating a small, community-focused institution.

Key limitations include extremely limited physical presence with only one branch and zero ATMs, which may create access challenges for members. The institution's membership is restricted to professional associations and trade organizations rather than being open to the general public. Service availability is currently limited with the lobby reported as closed during the research period, though online banking and mobile services remain operational. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Auto loans for new and used vehicles
pros: Member-owned not-for-profit structure with competitive rates and lower fees than commercial banks
Comprehensive product suite including savings, loans, mortgages, CDs, and IRAs
Online banking and mobile app available for iOS and Android devices
Online bill pay and financial calculators for member convenience
Specializes in professional associations and trade organizations with dedicated focus
Established 60+ years with stable operations since 1964
Financial education resources available to members
cons: Only one physical branch location with zero ATMs, severely limiting in-person access
Membership restricted to professional associations and trade organizations only—not open to general public
Very small institution ($2.8M assets, 300+ members) limits resources and product development
Lobby currently closed, reducing physical banking accessibility
Limited information on mortgage lending specifics despite offering mortgages
best_for: Members of professional associations and trade organizations seeking member-owned banking
Borrowers prioritizing personal loans and auto financing through a credit union structure
Organizations looking for financial services tailored to their membership base
diagnosis: New England Lee is exclusively for members of professional associations and trade organizations—not a general-purpose credit union for retail consumers. The single branch location and zero ATMs create significant access challenges despite solid product offerings, making it viable only for members with digital banking comfort and proximity to Boston.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
